description: Room, area, vnum, exits, world time, and room table fixtures.

origin.source: Constructed from PRD source protocol facts for confirmed room identity MSDP variables.

fixtures.description: Room name, area name, and room vnum parse as scalar values.
sourceFacts: PRD Source Protocol Facts confirms ROOM_NAME, AREA_NAME, and ROOM_VNUM.
Session 02 maps roomName, areaName, and roomVnum as first-class MudState fields.
notes: Room labels are synthetic and avoid live area capture content.

origin.source: Constructed from PRD source protocol facts for confirmed ROOM_EXITS.

fixtures.description: ROOM_EXITS parses as a table with mixed numeric destination ids and string status values.
sourceFacts: PRD Source Protocol Facts confirms ROOM_EXITS is populated by source and should support room/map work.
notes: The current parser preserves table shape as an object.

origin.source: Constructed from PRD source protocol facts for confirmed ROOM structured data.

fixtures.description: ROOM parses as a structured table for future raw room payload coverage.
sourceFacts: PRD Source Protocol Facts confirms ROOM is populated by source.
Session 02 keeps structured MSDP payloads as MudValue until shapes are confirmed.
notes: Lowercase table keys are synthetic parser keys and are not a source-level shape guarantee.

origin.source: Constructed from PRD source protocol facts for confirmed WORLD_TIME.

fixtures.description: WORLD_TIME parses as a string because the client currently treats it as optional text.
sourceFacts: PRD Source Protocol Facts confirms WORLD_TIME.
shared/mud.ts maps worldTime to an optional string.
notes: The text value is synthetic and used only for parser and mapping coverage.

origin.source: Constructed from Session 05 room display partial-state requirements.

fixtures.description: Partial room identity values keep zero and blank values distinct from missing data.
sourceFacts: PRD Source Protocol Facts confirms ROOM_NAME, AREA_NAME, ROOM_VNUM, and WORLD_TIME.
Session 05 requires zero vnum and blank text to stay distinct from missing room fields.
notes: Blank strings are synthetic and verify UI empty-state handling without live MUD access.

origin.source: Constructed from Session 05 conservative ROOM_EXITS fallback requirements.

fixtures.description: ROOM_EXITS can arrive as a scalar string and must remain available for raw fallback display.
sourceFacts: PRD Source Protocol Facts confirms ROOM_EXITS.
Session 05 requires string exit payloads to render without inventing mapper semantics.
notes: Exit labels are synthetic and do not prove final source exit formatting.

origin.source: Constructed from Session 05 array exit display requirements.

fixtures.description: ROOM_EXITS can be represented as an array of direction labels.
sourceFacts: PRD Source Protocol Facts confirms ROOM_EXITS.
Phase 01 parser coverage supports MSDP arrays.
notes: Array direction labels are representative display inputs only.

origin.source: Constructed from Session 05 empty room context requirements.

fixtures.description: Explicit empty ROOM and ROOM_EXITS values remain present data, not missing variables.
sourceFacts: PRD Source Protocol Facts confirms ROOM and ROOM_EXITS.
Session 05 requires empty room context to stay distinct from waiting, disabled, offline, and error states.
notes: Empty structures are synthetic parser contracts for display-state tests.

origin.source: Constructed from Session 05 object-like exit display requirements.

fixtures.description: ROOM_EXITS object-like rows preserve destination, status, and unknown fields for bounded summaries.
sourceFacts: PRD Source Protocol Facts confirms ROOM_EXITS.
Session 05 requires unknown exit fields to be surfaced conservatively.
notes: Object keys are synthetic and verify deterministic display behavior, not final source schema.

origin.source: Constructed from Session 05 structured ROOM fallback requirements.

fixtures.description: ROOM partial table data can supply identity fields and bounded unknown-field text.
sourceFacts: PRD Source Protocol Facts confirms ROOM.
Session 05 requires structured ROOM payloads to remain secondary to terminal room prose.
notes: Unknown fields are synthetic and should be displayed as bounded secondary details.

origin.source: Constructed from Session 05 malformed/raw fallback requirements.

fixtures.description: A malformed-looking ROOM_EXITS scalar remains a bounded raw fallback value.
sourceFacts: PRD Source Protocol Facts confirms ROOM_EXITS.
Session 05 requires malformed or uncertain exit values to remain visible without parser crashes.
notes: This is a display fallback fixture; byte-level malformed MSDP coverage remains in malformed-payloads.json.
